title: Type remotes config like TargetsConfig
description: config.rs:81 — Remotes stored as BTreeMap<String, serde_json::Value>, parsed dynamically with string matching. Inconsistent with fully-typed TargetsConfig. Consider a RemotesConfig struct with Option<T> fields, or at minimum test coverage ensuring validate_remotes() covers all known remote names.

title: Empty string value detection and warnings
description:
Detect and warn about empty string secret values across esk commands.

## Motivation

t3-env added `emptyStringAsUndefined` because empty string env vars are a major foot-gun in production. When a .env file has `PORT=` (no value), `process.env.PORT` is `""` — not `undefined`. This breaks type coercion, prevents defaults from applying, and causes silent failures.

The same problem exists at the secrets management layer. A user might accidentally `esk set PORT ""` or an import/sync might bring in empty values. Currently esk stores and deploys these silently.

## Proposed behavior

### `esk set KEY ""` or `esk set KEY "   "`

```
⚠ Warning: Setting empty value for PORT. Continue? [y/N]
```

Interactive prompt (skip with `--force` or when not a TTY). Still allows it — some secrets legitimately need empty values as "unset" markers.

### `esk status`

Add a section for empty value warnings:

```
⚠ Empty values detected:
  PORT (dev) — empty string
  API_KEY (staging) — whitespace only
```

### `esk deploy`

Warn before deploying empty values:

```
⚠ Warning: Deploying empty values:
  PORT → fly (dev)
  API_KEY → vercel (staging)
Continue? [y/N]
```

Skip with `--force`. In non-interactive mode (CI), fail by default (require `--allow-empty` to proceed).

### `esk sync`

After pull, report any empty values received from remote:

```
ℹ Pulled 5 secrets from 1password (prod)
⚠ 1 empty value received: DEPRECATED_KEY
```

## Implementation notes

- Add `is_empty_or_whitespace(value: &str) -> bool` utility
- Hook into `set` command before store write
- Hook into `deploy` command during pre-deploy validation pass
- Hook into `status` command as a new diagnostic section
- Hook into `sync` command after reconciliation
- All warnings should be suppressible via `--force` or `--allow-empty` flags
- Consider a config-level setting: `allow_empty: true` per secret for intentional empty values
- Respect TTY detection (atty crate already in use) — in non-interactive mode, empty values should be errors not prompts

## Edge cases

- Whitespace-only values (`"   "`, `"\t"`, `"\n"`) should be treated as empty
- Values that are just quotes (`'""'`, `"''"`) should NOT be flagged — those are intentional
- Tombstoned (deleted) secrets should not be flagged

## Prior art

- t3-env: `emptyStringAsUndefined` option strips empty strings before validation so defaults apply correctly
- arkenv: does not handle this explicitly (relies on ArkType validation to reject empty strings for non-string types)
close_reason: Implemented empty string value detection and warnings across set, deploy, status, and sync commands

title: Add ReconcileAction::RepairDrift variant
description: ReconcileAction::PushLocal currently has two distinct meanings: (a) local version is higher, push diff, and (b) versions are equal but content drifted, push repair. Split into PushLocal (version wins) and RepairDrift (equal version, content mismatch) for better observability and distinct UI messages.
close_reason: Added RepairDrift variant, updated Equal branch, derived PartialEq/Eq/Clone/Copy, migrated all tests from matches! to assert_eq!

title: Fix reconcile env version fallback inflating local version for unknown envs
close_reason: Replaced inline .unwrap_or(local.version) with StorePayload::env_version() in both reconcile() and reconcile_multi(). Added regression tests for the data-loss scenario.

title: Zeroize serialized JSON before encryption
description: Zeroize all plaintext secrets and key material in store.rs memory paths. Four locations fixed: (1) write_payload JSON serialization, (2) decrypt return value, (3) read_key_file hex string (master key), (4) write_key_file hex encoding (master key). Also added RFC 5869 doc comment to derive_key explaining why None salt is correct.
close_reason: Resolved as part of esk-7pk security review

title: Set 0o600 permissions on deploy-index.json and sync-index.json
description: deploy_tracker.rs:78-89 — Index files written without restricted permissions. They contain SHA-256 hashes that could enable offline brute-force of low-entropy secrets. Set 0o600 consistent with store.enc and store.key.
close_reason: Closed

title: Security & architecture review remediation
description: Parent epic for all findings from the security audit and principal engineer architecture review conducted 2026-03-01. Covers cryptographic hardening, memory safety for secrets, deploy orchestration refactoring, and config model improvements.

title: generate: Zod schema output format
Required-variable auditing per deploy target
description:
Track which variables each deploy target requires and flag missing ones in status/deploy.

## Motivation

t3-env ships platform presets for Vercel, Railway, Fly, Supabase, Netlify, Render, Neon, UploadThing, and Upstash Redis. Each preset declares which env vars the platform provides or expects. This catches the common failure mode: deploying to a new environment but forgetting to set a critical variable.

esk already knows which adapters are configured and which secrets target which adapters. The missing piece is knowing which secrets are REQUIRED vs optional for each target, and surfacing gaps before deploy.

## Proposed config syntax

### Per-secret requirement declarations

```yaml
secrets:
  DATABASE_URL:
    targets: [fly, vercel]
    required: true              # required for all targeted environments (default)
    
  ANALYTICS_KEY:
    targets: [vercel]
    required: false             # optional — no warning if missing
    
  SENTRY_DSN:
    targets: [fly, vercel]
    required:
      environments: [prod, staging]  # required only in specific environments
```

### Per-adapter required variables

Adapters can declare variables they always need:

```yaml
adapters:
  fly:
    app: my-app
    required_secrets: [DATABASE_URL, SECRET_KEY_BASE]  # must be set before deploy
    
  vercel:
    project: my-project
    required_secrets: [NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL]
```

## Interaction with default values

Secrets with a `default` value (from esk-cyx validation schema) are effectively always present — they satisfy the required check even when no value is stored. The precedence is:

1. Stored value exists → required satisfied
2. No stored value, default exists → required satisfied (deploy uses default)
3. No stored value, no default, `required: true` → error
4. No stored value, no default, `required: false` → skip silently

This means adding `default: "development"` to NODE_ENV implicitly makes it non-blocking for required checks, without needing `required: false`.

## Enforcement points

### `esk status`

```
Environment: prod
  ✓ fly: 12/12 secrets deployed, all required present
  ⚠ vercel: 10/11 secrets deployed
    Missing required: NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL

Environment: staging
  ✓ fly: 12/12 secrets deployed, all required present
  ✓ vercel: 11/11 secrets deployed, all required present
  
Environment: dev
  ⚠ fly: 8/12 secrets deployed
    Missing required: DATABASE_URL, SECRET_KEY_BASE
    Missing optional: ANALYTICS_KEY, SENTRY_DSN
```

### `esk deploy`

Before deploying, check that all required secrets for the target environment have values (stored or default):

```
✗ Cannot deploy to fly (prod): missing required secrets:
  DATABASE_URL — no value set for prod (no default)
  SECRET_KEY_BASE — no value set for prod (no default)

Set them with:
  esk set DATABASE_URL --env prod
  esk set SECRET_KEY_BASE --env prod
```

Deploy should fail by default when required secrets are missing. Override with `--force` for partial deploys.

### `esk deploy --dry-run`

Should include requirement checks in its output:

```
Dry run: fly (prod)
  Would deploy: API_KEY, NODE_ENV (default: development), PORT (default: 3000) (3 secrets)
  ⚠ Missing required: DATABASE_URL, SECRET_KEY_BASE
```

## Implementation notes

- Add `required` field to secret config in config.rs (default: true for backwards compatibility, or could default to false to be non-breaking — need to decide)
- Add optional `required_secrets` field to adapter config
- Create a `requirements.rs` module or add to existing status/deploy logic
- Requirement checking should happen BEFORE any deployment starts
- When checking requirements, consult both the store (stored values) and validation config (defaults) to determine if a secret has an effective value
- Combine with validation schemas (esk-cyx) — validate + require check in one pre-deploy pass
- Consider generating a "deployment readiness" report combining all checks

## Prior art

- t3-env platform presets: declare which env vars each platform provides/expects. E.g., the Vercel preset marks VERCEL_URL, VERCEL_ENV as provided (optional) and framework-specific vars as user-required
- t3-env's strict mode (`runtimeEnvStrict`): requires explicitly listing every env var — missing one is a type error
- arkenv: doesn't have platform-specific presets but all declared vars are required by default (fail on missing). Supports optional keys via `?` suffix on key name
design:
## Refined design (PE-reviewed)

### Config changes

Per-secret `required` field on SecretDef. No `required_secrets` on targets.

```yaml
secrets:
  DATABASE_URL:
    targets: [fly, vercel]
    # required: true  (default when omitted)
    
  ANALYTICS_KEY:
    targets: [vercel]
    required: false              # optional
    
  SENTRY_DSN:
    targets: [fly, vercel]
    required: [prod, staging]    # required in specific envs only
```

### Required enum

```rust
enum Required { All, None, Environments(Vec<String>) }
```

- Absent → `All` (default true — safe default)
- `false` → `None`
- `true` → `All`
- `[prod, staging]` → `Environments(vec!["prod", "staging"])`

Custom Deserialize impl (not untagged) for clear error messages.

### check_requirements()

Method on Config returning `Vec<MissingRequirement>`. Pure function, no separate module.
Forward-compatible with defaults (esk-9f9) — check store first, then default (always None until defaults land).

### Enforcement

- **deploy**: Fail unless `--force`. `--dry-run` warns only. Check after validation, before deploy work.
- **status**: Show missing-required section with next-step suggestions.
- **delete**: Warn + confirm when deleting a required secret.

### Implementation phases

1. Config schema — Required enum, SecretDef field, ResolvedSecret propagation, env validation
2. Requirements checking — check_requirements() on Config
3. Deploy integration — pre-deploy check, --force bypass, --dry-run warning
4. Status integration — missing-required section, next steps
5. Delete warning — confirm prompt on required secret deletion
6. Tests — ~20 unit + integration tests
close_reason: Already fully implemented: Required enum, check_requirements(), deploy/status/delete integration, 16 unit+integration tests, REQUIRED_CONFIG fixture — all design phases complete

title: Return Zeroizing<Vec<u8>> from key-producing functions
description: store.rs:265-268 — generate_key(), read_key_file(), and KeyProvider::load() return plain Vec<u8>. Intermediate key material not zeroed on drop. Return Zeroizing<Vec<u8>> from all three.

title: Fix is_gitignored substring matching in generate command
description: is_gitignored in src/cli/generate.rs uses filename.contains(pattern) which is backwards. Glob patterns like *.ts never match, while single-char entries like 'e' match everything. Either use the ignore crate or simplify to exact line matching. Only affects a cosmetic hint but actively gives wrong advice.
close_reason: Fixed substring matching: extracted pattern_matches() with exact/leading-glob/trailing-glob support, match against both filename and full path. Added 3 tests.

title: Use Zeroizing<Vec<u8>> for SecretStore.key
description: store.rs:304 — SecretStore.key is plain Vec<u8> with manual Drop. Reallocation or panics can leak key material. Change to Zeroizing<Vec<u8>> and remove the manual Drop impl. Already using Zeroizing for derived keys at line 602.

title: generate: --env flag for environment-scoped output
description:
Every other esk command accepts --env to scope to a specific environment. The generate command should too.

Currently all secret keys across all environments are unioned into a flat namespace. With --env, only keys that have targets in that environment would be included. Useful when different environments have different secret sets.

Example:
  esk generate --env prod          # only secrets targeting prod
  esk generate --env dev --runtime # runtime validator for dev secrets only

title: Security: low priority findings
description: Deploy/sync index file permissions, SecretValue missing ZeroizeOnDrop, cloud file cleartext format missing warning.
close_reason: All child issues resolved: esk-1a8 (file permissions) and esk-k9r (cleartext warning) implemented, esk-sdm (ZeroizeOnDrop) closed as won't-fix

title: Dedupe env_payload_version into StorePayload method
description: Identical function exists in both src/cli/sync.rs:14 (env_payload_version) and src/cli/status.rs:687 (local_env_version). Should be a method on StorePayload or a shared helper in store.rs. If the semantics of env version ever change, both copies must be found and updated independently.
close_reason: Deduped into StorePayload::env_version() method with unit tests

title: Migrate remaining adapter/plugin tests to shared test_support harness
close_reason: Closed

title: Add --prefer local|remote flag to esk sync
description: Currently equal-version conflicts silently treat local as source-of-truth. Add a --prefer flag to esk sync that controls who wins ties. --prefer local (default, current behavior) and --prefer remote (remote wins at equal version). This gives teams explicit control over conflict resolution direction.
close_reason: Implemented --prefer local|remote flag for esk sync

title: Orphaned secret detection (undeclared variable audit)
description:
Detect secrets deployed to targets that are no longer declared in esk.yaml config.

## Motivation

arkenv defaults `onUndeclaredKey` to `"delete"` — stripping env vars from the output that aren't in the schema. This prevents accidental leakage of sensitive values and catches config drift where a secret was removed from the schema but never cleaned up in the target.

esk faces the same problem: when a secret is removed from esk.yaml (or its target is removed), the previously-deployed value remains in the target platform (Vercel, Fly, etc.) indefinitely. These orphaned secrets are a security risk (stale credentials that should have been rotated) and a maintenance burden (confusion about what's actually in use).

## Proposed behavior

### `esk status --orphans`

Compare the sync index (what was deployed) against the current config (what should be deployed):

```
Orphaned secrets (deployed but no longer in config):
  OLD_DATABASE_URL → fly (prod) — deployed 2025-12-15
  DEPRECATED_API_KEY → vercel (prod, staging) — deployed 2025-11-03
  REMOVED_FEATURE_FLAG → fly (dev) — deployed 2026-01-20

These secrets exist in deploy targets but are not declared in esk.yaml.
Clean up with: esk deploy --prune
```

### `esk deploy --prune`

Actively remove orphaned secrets from targets during deploy:

```
Pruning orphaned secrets:
  ✓ Deleted OLD_DATABASE_URL from fly (prod)
  ✓ Deleted DEPRECATED_API_KEY from vercel (prod)
  ✓ Deleted DEPRECATED_API_KEY from vercel (staging)
  ✓ Deleted REMOVED_FEATURE_FLAG from fly (dev)
```

This should require confirmation (interactive prompt) and support `--dry-run`.

### `esk status` (default view)

Add a count to the normal status output:

```
Environment: prod
  fly: 12 secrets deployed, 2 orphaned
  vercel: 10 secrets deployed, 1 orphaned
  
Run `esk status --orphans` for details.
```

## Detection logic

1. Load sync index (`.esk/sync-index.json`) — records of all previous deploys
2. Load current config (`esk.yaml`) — current secret + target declarations
3. For each record in sync index where `status == "success"`:
   - Check if the secret key still exists in config
   - Check if the secret still targets this adapter
   - Check if the secret still targets this environment
   - If any check fails → mark as orphaned
4. Report orphaned secrets grouped by target and environment

## Implementation notes

- Add `--orphans` flag to status command
- Add `--prune` flag to deploy command
- Orphan detection is a comparison between sync index records and current config state
- Pruning uses existing `delete_secret()` adapter methods (already implemented for individual adapters)
- Batch adapters (env_file, kubernetes) handle orphan removal implicitly when they regenerate the full output — but should still report the orphans
- After pruning, remove the orphaned records from sync index
- Consider a `--prune` safety check: require `--force` to prune more than N secrets at once to prevent accidental mass deletion from a config mistake
- Should NOT prune secrets that failed their last deploy (they may have never actually been deployed)

## Edge cases

- Config was accidentally truncated — pruning would delete everything. The N-secret safety threshold helps here
- Secret was renamed (old removed, new added) — shows as orphan + new. Could detect rename patterns but probably not worth the complexity
- Multiple environments with different secret sets — orphan detection must be per-environment
- Secrets deployed by a previous version of esk that didn't track in sync index — cannot detect these

## Prior art

- arkenv: `onUndeclaredKey: "delete"` strips undeclared keys from output by default, `"reject"` throws errors on unknown keys
- t3-env: Proxy blocks access to undeclared vars (server/client separation), but doesn't detect/remove them from the platform
- Terraform: `terraform plan` shows resources to destroy when removed from config — same concept applied to secrets
design:
## Implementation plan

### Architecture

**Data source**: Deploy index (`.esk/deploy-index.json`) — records every deployment as `(key, target, app, env)` tuples with value hashes. Orphan detection is a set-difference: deploy index keys minus expected keys from current config.

**Tombstone handling**: Skip records with `TOMBSTONE_HASH` + `Success` (already cleaned up). Include `TOMBSTONE_HASH` + `Failed` (deletion attempted but failed — secret may still exist on target).

### File changes

1. **New: `src/orphan.rs`** — Pure detection function. `TargetOrphan` struct + `detect_target_orphans(index, config, env_filter)`. Set-difference algorithm.

2. **Modified: `src/cli/mod.rs` + `src/main.rs`** — Add `--prune` flag to Deploy variant.

3. **Modified: `src/cli/deploy.rs`** — Prune logic after normal deploy: detect orphans → safety threshold (10, require `--force`) → confirmation prompt → individual targets: `delete_secret()` per orphan → batch targets: force-dirty group for regeneration → remove orphan records from deploy index.

4. **Modified: `src/cli/status.rs`** — Add 'Target orphans' section to dashboard, distinct from store orphans. Count in summary + `esk deploy --prune` suggestion.

5. **Modified: `src/deploy_tracker.rs`** — Add `remove_record()` method.

6. **Modified: `src/lib.rs`** — Add `pub mod orphan;`

### Design decisions

- `--prune` as deploy flag (not separate subcommand) — atomic deploy + cleanup in one pass
- `--prune` requires `--env` — safer for destructive operation
- Unavailable targets reported as 'cannot prune: target unavailable' rather than silently skipped
- Store orphans remain separate concern (`esk delete`)
- Remove orphan records from index entirely after pruning (no tombstones)
- Safety threshold: 10 orphans requires `--force`

### Testing

Unit tests (8 cases in orphan.rs): no orphans, secret removed, target removed, tombstone success excluded, tombstone failure included, env filter, empty index, empty config.

Integration tests: --prune --dry-run, prune with individual target, prune with batch target, safety threshold exceeded, status shows orphans.

title: Add animated preflight rendering for remotes
description: remotes/mod.rs:242-269 vs targets/mod.rs:680-722 — Targets get animated spinners for preflight; remotes get silent cliclack::log::warning. Users may miss skipped remotes, especially in CI. Apply consistent preflight UX or at minimum show a pass/fail summary.

title: generate: framework presets
Architecture: medium priority findings
description: TargetsConfig manual registry, untyped remotes config, silent remote preflight failures, tombstone growth.

title: Fix orphan detection issues from PE review
description:
PE review of commit 7fee8e3 found several issues in the orphan detection/pruning feature:

CRITICAL:
1. Tracker key parsing in orphan.rs:55-58 duplicates format knowledge from DeployIndex::tracker_key(). Add parse_tracker_key() as canonical inverse.
2. No test for --prune without --env (multi-env prune path unexercised).

CONCERNS:
3. Batch prune removes index records even if batch deploy fails - could leave stale secrets with no tracking.
4. env_status is an unwieldy 4-tuple (deployed, failed, unset, pruned) - use a struct.
5. target_display formatting repeated 3 times (deploy.rs:303, 824, status.rs:447) - add TargetOrphan::target_display().

MINOR:
6. index.save() called on every loop iteration including failure paths (wasted I/O).
7. Safety threshold of 10 is hardcoded (fine for now, consider configurable later).

title: Idiomatic cleanup: CommandOutput::check(), delete append_env_flags, Config methods, Options structs
description: Targeted cleanup per docs/idiomatic-cleanup-spec.md. Eight changes: (1) CommandOutput::check() method — 27 sites, (2) delete append_env_flags, use .extend() — 24 sites, (3) delete StatusTheme/ListTheme dupes, (4) unify relative_time, (5) Config::find_and_load(), (6) Config::validate_env(), (7) sync::run_with_runner accepts SyncOptions, (8) set::run gets SetOptions. All idiomatic Rust — no new abstractions.
close_reason: All 8 idiomatic cleanup changes implemented: CommandOutput::check(), delete append_env_flags, delete StatusTheme/ListTheme, unify relative_time, Config::find_and_load(), Config::validate_env(), SyncOptions struct, SetOptions struct. 775 tests pass, clippy clean.

title: Security: medium priority findings
description: env_flags whitespace splitting, secrets as CLI args exposure, plaintext JSON before encryption not zeroed, HKDF without salt.
close_reason: All 4 medium-priority findings resolved: esk-19l fixed (4 zeroization gaps in store.rs), esk-9qj wontfix (RFC 5869 compliant), esk-a1j already mitigated (runtime warnings exist), esk-wtv not a security issue (usability, not injection)

title: Design and implement default: values for secret schemas
description: Follow-up from esk-cyx validation schemas. Key design questions: tombstone + default interaction model, deploy tracker hash for config-sourced values, esk list / esk status display of defaulted secrets. Defaults create a third semantic state that leaks into tombstones, reconciliation, deploy tracking, and sync — significant complexity that warrants separate design.

title: Add application-specific salt to HKDF
description: store.rs:601 — HKDF uses None for salt. While acceptable per RFC 5869 with uniform IKM, a fixed app-specific salt like b"esk-hkdf-v1" provides defense-in-depth against weak RNG.
close_reason: Resolved as part of esk-7pk security review

title: Add tombstone pruning / GC strategy
description: StorePayload tombstones are never pruned. High-turnover projects will bloat the encrypted store over time. Implement GC — e.g., prune tombstones older than the oldest remote's version, or add an explicit prune command.

title: Document CLI-arg secret exposure for netlify/heroku/azure/gcp targets
description: netlify.rs:57, heroku.rs:71 and others pass secrets as CLI arguments visible in ps aux. Already has code-level warnings. Add user-facing documentation about this limitation. Consider a --no-process-leak flag to skip these targets.
close_reason: Resolved as part of esk-7pk security review

title: Expand integration surface: new target and remote candidates
description: Track evaluation and implementation of additional deploy targets and sync remotes identified in candidate review.

title: Add target: Render
description: Child issue under esk-c72 for candidate integration planning and implementation.
notes: PRIORITY ASSESSMENT (Tier 1, Rank #2): 4.5M developers, $1.5B valuation, growing fast (250K+ new users/month). No CLI for env vars — requires REST API (per-service PUT or per-key PATCH). Would need HTTP client (ureq) or curl via CommandRunner. Worth it given adoption trajectory. Architectural decision: first target requiring direct HTTP calls.

title: Add remote: Keeper
description: Child issue under esk-c72 for candidate integration planning and implementation.
notes: Priority rationale: Legitimate enterprise player but integration friction is high — secrets addressed by UID not name, structured records (not flat KV), writes need folder UID + record type. Audience skews enterprise IT, not developer teams. Doable but awkward. Build later, lower priority than Akeyless.

title: Add remote: Akeyless
description: Child issue under esk-c72 for candidate integration planning and implementation.
notes: Priority rationale: Growing enterprise adoption, named KuppingerCole 'Overall Leader' 2025. Clean CLI (akeyless get-secret-value/create-secret), path-based secrets map well. Captures teams migrating off self-managed Vault after BSL relicense. SaaS-first, zero-ops. Build later.

title: Add remote: OCI Vault
description: Child issue under esk-c72 for candidate integration planning and implementation.
notes: Priority rationale: OCI holds ~3% cloud market share. CLI is rough — every operation requires 60+ char OCIDs, split vault/secrets namespaces, no instant delete (24h minimum scheduling). Only valuable for Oracle-ecosystem shops. Backlog.

title: Add remote: Consul KV
description: Child issue under esk-c72 for candidate integration planning and implementation.
close_reason: Won't implement. Consul KV has no encryption at rest — values stored in plaintext. HashiCorp's own guidance is to use Vault for secrets, not Consul KV. esk already supports HashiCorp Vault. Adding Consul KV would create a false sense of security.

title: Add remote: Azure App Configuration
description: Evaluate and implement Azure App Configuration as a sync remote. Prefer per-environment snapshot storage with explicit version metadata to align with existing remote reconciliation model.
close_reason: Won't implement. Azure App Configuration is not a secrets store — it stores plaintext config and Key Vault references. The existing azure_key_vault remote already covers Azure secrets sync. Adding this would be misleading since esk payloads contain real secret values.

title: Investigate OS keychain-backed local secret key storage
description: Design support for storing local encryption material in OS secure stores (macOS Keychain, Linux libsecret, Windows Credential Manager). This is likely a local store/key-provider feature rather than a sync remote.
notes: Priority rationale: Real security improvement that benefits all users — eliminates .esk/store.key file from disk (no accidental git commit, no backup leakage). macOS Secure Enclave support via keyring crate (v3.6+, 3700+ dependents). Graceful fallback to key file for CI/CD and headless. Not a remote — a core enhancement. Build now.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add remote: Upstash/Redis
description: Evaluate and implement a Redis-backed sync remote (including Upstash). Store per-environment payload snapshots with version metadata; document persistence/eviction requirements and key namespace conventions.
notes: Priority rationale: Redis is not a secrets manager — no audit, no versioning, no rotation. Upstash is popular in serverless niche (~85k devs) but GET/SET on a dumb blob store adds little over existing remotes. Only useful for teams wanting quick shared config. Backlog unless user demand materializes.

title: Add target: AWS Lambda
description: Deploy secrets directly to Lambda function environment configuration (without requiring runtime pull from SSM). Ensure merge-safe updates that do not clobber unrelated env vars.
notes: PRIORITY ASSESSMENT (Tier 1, Rank #1): ~29% serverless market share, clear market leader. Batch target — aws lambda update-function-configuration replaces full env var map (read-merge-write). Clean CLI integration via aws CLI. Huge audience. Config: function name + optional region/kms_key_arn.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add target: Expo EAS
description: Deploy secrets to Expo Application Services for mobile build/runtime workflows. Validate support for environment scoping and CI-friendly auth.
notes: PRIORITY ASSESSMENT (Tier 3, Rank #12): Dominant in React Native but narrow audience. eas env:create — no upsert (try-create/fallback-update), no stdin for values. Environment name mapping needed (dev→development, prod→production). Individual target. Config: project + env name mapping.

title: Add target: Terraform Cloud workspace variables
description: Deploy secrets as Terraform Cloud workspace variables with clear handling for sensitivity flags and environment/workspace mapping.
notes: PRIORITY ASSESSMENT (Tier 2, Rank #9): Dominant IaC platform ($6.4B acquisition). No CLI for workspace vars — REST API only (POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/v2/workspaces/:id/vars). Breaks CommandRunner pattern like Render/Bitbucket. Clean conceptual fit for deploying provider credentials. Config: workspace-id + TFE_TOKEN.

title: Add target: DigitalOcean App Platform
description: Child issue under esk-c72 for candidate integration planning and implementation.
notes: PRIORITY ASSESSMENT (Tier 3, Rank #11): ~600K customers but App Platform is niche PaaS. Full app spec replacement (read-modify-write). Community reports env vars getting silently dropped on spec updates — fragile. Batch target via doctl apps update --spec. Config: app-id.

title: Evaluate Pulumi ESC integration
description: Evaluate integration with Pulumi ESC for secret/config consumption. Decide whether this should be modeled as a deploy target, sync remote, or both.
notes: PRIORITY ASSESSMENT (Deprioritize): Pulumi ESC is a competing product (cloud-hosted secrets aggregation), not a natural deploy target. Low adoption ('thousands of developers'). Better modeled as a remote (like Doppler) if anything — bidirectional sync via esc CLI. Not worth a target implementation.

title: Add target: Bitbucket Pipelines
description: Deploy secrets to Bitbucket Pipelines repository/workspace variables to complement existing CI target coverage.
notes: PRIORITY ASSESSMENT (Tier 2, Rank #7): ~18-19% CI/CD market share (Atlassian ecosystem). No CLI — REST API only. Three scope tiers (workspace/repo/deployment). Deployment env vars require UUID lookup. High friction but large installed base. Would need HTTP client or curl. Auth via app password or OAuth token.

title: Evaluate target: Auth0
description: Assess feasibility and value of a dedicated Auth0 target for provider-managed credentials/secrets and rotation workflows.
notes: PRIORITY ASSESSMENT (Drop): Auth0 is primarily a credential source, not a deploy target. Auth0 Actions secrets are technically writable via Management API but extremely niche, poorly supported by Auth0's own tooling (bugs in auth0-deploy-cli), and no simple CLI command. Close this issue.

title: Evaluate target: Stripe
description: Assess feasibility and value of a dedicated Stripe target for webhook/client-secret related workflows and rotation constraints.
notes: PRIORITY ASSESSMENT (Drop): Stripe is NOT a deploy target. It is a payment processor — secrets flow is one-directional (Stripe gives YOU API keys, you store them in other targets). No environment to push secrets to. No CLI mechanism for receiving configuration. Close this issue.

title: Add target: Firebase Functions
description: Deploy secrets/environment configuration to Firebase Functions with environment-aware mapping and safe update semantics.
notes: PRIORITY ASSESSMENT (Tier 2, Rank #10): Widely used in mobile dev (72% iOS integration reach). firebase functions:secrets:set reads from stdin. But secrets are GCP Secret Manager under the hood — arguably already covered by existing gcp_secret_manager remote. Narrow audience overlap. Config: project-id.

title: Add target: GCP Cloud Run
description: Child issue under esk-c72 for candidate integration planning and implementation.
notes: PRIORITY ASSESSMENT (Tier 1, Rank #3): Google's serverless containers. gcloud run services update --update-env-vars for plain env vars (individual deploy, Heroku-like pattern). Secret Manager integration possible but more complex (two-step). Start with plain env vars. Config: service name + project + region.
close_reason: Implemented GCP Cloud Run deploy target with config, target implementation, mod registration, test fixture, and integration tests. All 14 new tests pass.

title: Add target: Azure App Service
description: Child issue under esk-c72 for candidate integration planning and implementation.
notes: PRIORITY ASSESSMENT (Tier 1, Rank #4): ~2,900+ companies, dominant Azure PaaS. az webapp config appsettings set/delete — clean individual target. Values passed as CLI args (no stdin). Enterprise audience. Config: resource-group + app-name. Slot support optional.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add target: Azure Container Apps
description: Child issue under esk-c72 for candidate integration planning and implementation.
notes: PRIORITY ASSESSMENT (Tier 2, Rank #6): Growing fast as Azure's container PaaS. az containerapp secret set/remove. Two-layer model: secrets + env var references (secretref:) adds complexity vs App Service's flat model. Values as CLI args (no stdin). 20-char key name limit. Config: resource-group + app-name.

title: Add target: CircleCI
description: Child issue under esk-c72 for candidate integration planning and implementation.
notes: PRIORITY ASSESSMENT (Tier 1, Rank #5): ~6,900 companies, 22K+ orgs. circleci context store-secret supports stdin (clean secret passing). Complements existing GitHub Actions target for CI/CD secrets. Two scopes: project vars vs contexts — contexts are the better target. Config: org-id + context-name.
close_reason: Implemented CircleCI deploy target with config, target impl, wiring, 8 unit tests, 5 integration tests

title: Add target: Buildkite
description: Child issue under esk-c72 for candidate integration planning and implementation.
notes: PRIORITY ASSESSMENT (Tier 3, Rank #13): Elite engineering orgs (Airbnb, Shopify, OpenAI) but only ~524 companies. Secrets are cluster-scoped (not per-pipeline). REST API for secret management, newer feature. Many users still use Vault/AWS SM plugins instead. Config: cluster-id + API token.

title: Add target: Azure DevOps
description: Child issue under esk-c72 for candidate integration planning and implementation.
notes: PRIORITY ASSESSMENT (Tier 2, Rank #8): ~24% CI/CD market share, enterprise-dominant. az pipelines variable-group variable create/update/delete. Clean secret passing via env var (AZURE_DEVOPS_EXT_PIPELINE_VAR_*). Variable groups are the right integration target. Config: org + project + group-id.

title: Add remote: Infisical
description: Child issue under esk-c72 for candidate integration planning and implementation.
notes: Priority rationale: 25k+ GitHub stars, MIT-licensed, self-hostable, YC-backed. Clean CLI (infisical secrets get/set/delete) maps directly to esk remote pattern — similar shape to existing Doppler remote. Fortune 500 adoption. Fastest-growing open-source secrets manager. Build now.
close_reason: Implemented Infisical remote with push (upsert + orphan deletion), pull (array-of-objects JSON), preflight, config, and 11 unit tests

title: generate: JSDoc comments from secret descriptions
Extract deploy.rs into plan/validate/execute/render phases
description: cli/deploy.rs is 1939 lines — validation, work planning, execution, and rendering all in one function. Extract into: plan_deploy(config, payload, index, opts) -> DeployPlan, validate_deploy(config, payload, plan, opts) -> Result<()>, execute_deploy(plan, targets, index, opts) -> DeployReport, render_deploy(report) -> Result<()>. Enables unit-testing individual phases.

title: Value validation schemas in esk.yaml
description:
Add optional validation rules per secret in esk.yaml, validated on `esk set` and `esk deploy`.

## Motivation

Both t3-env and arkenv's core value prop is catching bad values before they cause runtime failures. Currently esk accepts any string value for any secret — a typo'd URL or an empty port number silently deploys to production. Adding schema validation at the config layer catches these errors at the earliest possible point.

## Proposed config syntax

```yaml
secrets:
  DATABASE_URL:
    targets: [vercel, fly]
    validate:
      format: url
      
  PORT:
    targets: [fly]
    validate:
      format: integer
      range: [1, 65535]
      default: "3000"
      
  NODE_ENV:
    targets: [vercel, fly]
    validate:
      enum: [development, staging, production]
      default: development
      
  API_KEY:
    targets: [vercel]
    validate:
      format: string
      min_length: 32
      
  WEBHOOK_URL:
    targets: [vercel]
    validate:
      format: url
      pattern: "^https://"
      
  RATE_LIMIT:
    targets: [fly]
    validate:
      format: integer
      range: [1, 10000]
      
  ENABLE_FEATURE_X:
    targets: [vercel]
    validate:
      format: boolean
```

## Built-in format types

| Format    | Validates                                           |
|-----------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| `string`  | Any non-empty string (default if no format given)   |
| `url`     | Valid URL (scheme + host minimum)                   |
| `integer` | Parseable as i64                                    |
| `number`  | Parseable as f64                                    |
| `boolean` | One of: true, false, 1, 0, yes, no                 |
| `email`   | Basic email format check                            |
| `json`    | Valid JSON                                          |
| `base64`  | Valid base64-encoded string                         |

## Additional validation options

- `enum: [val1, val2, ...]` — value must be one of the listed strings
- `pattern: "regex"` — value must match the regex
- `min_length: N` / `max_length: N` — string length bounds
- `range: [min, max]` — numeric range (only with integer/number format)
- `optional: true` — value can be unset (no error if missing)
- `default: "value"` — default value when the secret is unset (see Default Values section)

## Default values

Inspired by arkenv's `= 'development'` syntax and t3-env's `.default()`. Secrets can declare a default value used when no value is stored.

### Behavior

- **`esk deploy`** — if a secret has no stored value but has a default, deploy the default. Skip the "missing required" error.
- **`esk generate --runtime`** — emit the default in generated code (e.g., `process.env.NODE_ENV ?? "development"`)
- **`esk generate` (.d.ts)** — no effect (types don't carry defaults)
- **`esk status`** — show "(default: development)" next to secrets using their default value
- **Env file target** — write the default value when no stored value exists
- **`esk set`** — stored values always override defaults. `esk delete` reverts to default (not missing)
- Defaults must pass the same validation rules as stored values (validated at config parse time)

### Interaction with required/optional

A secret with a `default` is implicitly optional for deploy purposes — it always has a value. Explicit `optional: true` means no error even without a default or stored value.

## Validation enforcement points

1. **`esk set KEY VALUE`** — validate immediately, reject with clear error before storing
2. **`esk deploy`** — validate all secrets for the target environment before deploying any. Fail fast with a summary of all validation errors, not just the first one
3. **`esk status`** — show validation warnings for currently stored values that don't pass

## Richer `esk generate` output

With type metadata available, `esk generate --runtime` should produce typed, validated code instead of bare existence checks.

### Current output (string-only, existence check)

```typescript
function requireEnv(key: string): string {
  const value = process.env[key];
  if (value === undefined || value === "") {
    throw new Error(`Missing required environment variable: ${key}`);
  }
  return value;
}

export const env = {
  DATABASE_URL: requireEnv("DATABASE_URL"),
  PORT: requireEnv("PORT"),
} as const;
```

### Proposed output (typed, coerced, defaults)

```typescript
function requireEnv(key: string): string {
  const value = process.env[key];
  if (value === undefined || value === "") {
    throw new Error(`Missing required environment variable: ${key}`);
  }
  return value;
}

function envInt(key: string, fallback?: number): number {
  const raw = process.env[key];
  if (raw === undefined || raw === "") {
    if (fallback !== undefined) return fallback;
    throw new Error(`Missing required environment variable: ${key}`);
  }
  const n = parseInt(raw, 10);
  if (isNaN(n)) throw new Error(`${key} must be an integer, got "${raw}"`);
  return n;
}

function envBool(key: string, fallback?: boolean): boolean {
  const raw = process.env[key];
  if (raw === undefined || raw === "") {
    if (fallback !== undefined) return fallback;
    throw new Error(`Missing required environment variable: ${key}`);
  }
  if (raw === "true" || raw === "1" || raw === "yes") return true;
  if (raw === "false" || raw === "0" || raw === "no") return false;
  throw new Error(`${key} must be a boolean, got "${raw}"`);
}

export const env = {
  DATABASE_URL: requireEnv("DATABASE_URL"),
  PORT: envInt("PORT", 3000),
  NODE_ENV: process.env.NODE_ENV ?? "development",
  ENABLE_FEATURE_X: envBool("ENABLE_FEATURE_X"),
} as const;
```

### Key improvements

- **Typed properties**: `PORT` is `number`, `ENABLE_FEATURE_X` is `boolean` — not everything `string`
- **Coercion helpers**: `envInt`, `envBool` parse and validate string env vars into proper JS types
- **Default values**: secrets with defaults use `?? "default"` or the `fallback` parameter
- **Optional secrets**: use `| undefined` in the type and skip the throw
- **Format-aware validation**: URL format could add a URL parse check, enum format could check against allowed values
- **.d.ts mode**: emit proper types (`PORT: number` instead of `PORT: string`) when format is integer/number/boolean

### Helpers to generate per format

| Format    | Helper function | Return type |
|-----------|----------------|-------------|
| `string`  | `requireEnv()` | `string`    |
| `url`     | `requireEnv()` (+ optional URL parse check) | `string` |
| `integer` | `envInt()`     | `number`    |
| `number`  | `envFloat()`   | `number`    |
| `boolean` | `envBool()`    | `boolean`   |
| `email`   | `requireEnv()` | `string`    |
| `json`    | `envJson()`    | `unknown`   |
| `base64`  | `requireEnv()` | `string`    |
| `enum`    | `requireEnv()` with union type | `"a" \| "b" \| "c"` |

## Implementation notes

- Add a `Validation` struct to config.rs with optional fields for each rule including `default`
- Parse validation rules in the secret config alongside existing `targets`, `environments` etc.
- Create a `validate.rs` module with a `validate_secret_value(key, value, rules) -> Result<(), Vec<ValidationError>>` function
- Validation should collect ALL errors per secret (not bail on first) for better DX
- The `validate` block should be entirely optional — secrets without it accept any string (backwards compatible)
- Consider a `--skip-validation` flag on deploy for emergency bypasses (similar to t3-env's `skipValidation`)
- No external crate dependencies needed — URL validation can use simple parsing, regex via the `regex` crate (already light)
- Update `cli/generate.rs` to read validation metadata and emit typed helpers
- Only emit helper functions that are actually used (don't generate `envBool` if no boolean secrets exist)

## Error output example

```
✗ Validation failed for environment "prod":
  DATABASE_URL: expected url format, got "not-a-url"
  PORT: expected integer in range [1, 65535], got "99999"
  NODE_ENV: expected one of [development, staging, production], got "prod"
```

## Prior art

- t3-env: Zod/Valibot/ArkType schemas per env var, validated at app startup. Recipes for coercion (booleans, numbers) are manual and error-prone. `emptyStringAsUndefined` handles the empty-string-vs-undefined footgun.
- arkenv: ArkType DSL strings with automatic coercion via JSON Schema introspection. `"0 <= number.integer <= 65535"` is concise but coupled to ArkType.
- Both validate at runtime; esk validates at deploy-time (earlier in the pipeline) AND generates the runtime code.
close_reason: Implemented validation schemas: Format enum (8 types), Validation struct, validate_value/validate_spec, enforcement in set/deploy/status, typed generate output, --skip-validation flag. Defaults deferred to esk-9f9.

title: Reduce ceremony for adding new targets
description: config.rs:96-140 — Adding a target requires touching TargetsConfig field, config struct, target_candidates(), and validate_remotes(). Consider a macro or declarative registration site. At minimum add a checklist comment documenting all touchpoints.

title: Architecture: low priority findings
description: dotenv dead code, CommandOutput error truncation, ReconcileOutcome type, Cloudflare mode enum.

title: Architecture: high priority findings
description: deploy.rs monolith extraction and vestigial StorePayload.version field.

title: generate: server/client variable separation
description:
t3-env's core feature is splitting env vars into server-only and client-safe buckets based on prefix (NEXT_PUBLIC_, NUXT_PUBLIC_, etc.).

Add a client_prefix option to generate config (in esk.yaml or CLI flag) that splits output into:
- Server types: all secrets NOT matching the prefix
- Client types: only secrets matching the prefix

Could generate separate files (env.server.d.ts / env.client.d.ts) or a single file with both namespaces. The runtime mode would generate two separate validated objects.

Config approach:
  generate:
    client_prefix: NEXT_PUBLIC_

CLI approach:
  esk generate --client-prefix NEXT_PUBLIC_

This prevents accidentally leaking server secrets to client bundles.

title: Warn when cloud_file remote uses cleartext format
description: remotes/cloud_file.rs:155-159 — format: cleartext stores secrets as plain JSON to cloud-synced directories. Log a warning when configured, or require explicit opt-in. Encrypted format (default) is already well-implemented.
close_reason: Closed

title: Conditional and cross-field validation rules
description:
Support validation rules that depend on the values of other secrets.

## Motivation

t3-env's `createFinalSchema` enables discriminated-union-style validation: "require EMAIL + PASSWORD unless SKIP_AUTH is true." Real-world secret configurations frequently have these interdependencies — an OAuth setup requires both client ID and client secret, a database connection needs all of host/port/user/password unless a connection string URL is provided, feature flags gate whether their associated config secrets are required.

Without cross-field validation, these invariants are only caught at runtime when the application fails to start.

## Proposed config syntax

### Simple dependency: `required_if`

```yaml
secrets:
  AUTH_ENABLED:
    targets: [fly]
    validate:
      format: boolean
      
  AUTH_SECRET:
    targets: [fly]
    validate:
      required_if:
        AUTH_ENABLED: "true"
        
  AUTH_ISSUER_URL:
    targets: [fly]
    validate:
      format: url
      required_if:
        AUTH_ENABLED: "true"
```

When `AUTH_ENABLED` is `"true"`, both `AUTH_SECRET` and `AUTH_ISSUER_URL` must be set. When `AUTH_ENABLED` is unset or `"false"`, they're optional.

### Mutual requirement: `required_with`

```yaml
secrets:
  OAUTH_CLIENT_ID:
    targets: [vercel]
    validate:
      required_with: [OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET]  # if one is set, both must be
      
  OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET:
    targets: [vercel]
    validate:
      required_with: [OAUTH_CLIENT_ID]
```

### Alternative: `required_unless`

```yaml
secrets:
  DATABASE_URL:
    targets: [fly]
    validate:
      format: url
      required_unless: [DB_HOST]  # not required if DB_HOST is set
      
  DB_HOST:
    targets: [fly]
    validate:
      required_unless: [DATABASE_URL]  # not required if DATABASE_URL is set
      
  DB_PORT:
    targets: [fly]
    validate:
      format: integer
      range: [1, 65535]
      required_if:
        DB_HOST: "*"  # required if DB_HOST has any value (wildcard)
        
  DB_USER:
    targets: [fly]
    validate:
      required_if:
        DB_HOST: "*"
        
  DB_PASSWORD:
    targets: [fly]
    validate:
      required_if:
        DB_HOST: "*"
```

This expresses: "Use either DATABASE_URL (connection string) OR the individual DB_HOST/PORT/USER/PASSWORD fields."

### Groups (stretch goal)

```yaml
validation_groups:
  database_url_mode:
    when:
      DATABASE_URL: "*"
    requires: []  # DATABASE_URL alone is sufficient
    
  database_components_mode:
    when:
      DB_HOST: "*"
    requires: [DB_PORT, DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD]
    
  database:
    one_of: [database_url_mode, database_components_mode]
```

This is more expressive but significantly more complex to implement. Consider as a v2 enhancement.

## Validation rule semantics

| Rule | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `required_if: { KEY: "value" }` | This secret is required when KEY equals "value" |
| `required_if: { KEY: "*" }` | This secret is required when KEY has any non-empty value |
| `required_with: [KEY1, KEY2]` | If this secret is set, all listed secrets must also be set (and vice versa) |
| `required_unless: [KEY1, KEY2]` | This secret is required UNLESS at least one of the listed secrets is set |

## Enforcement points

Same as value validation (esk set, esk deploy, esk status). Cross-field rules are only evaluable when the full secret set is available, so:

- `esk set` — can only check cross-field rules if all referenced secrets exist in the store. Warn if a dependency is missing but don't block (the user might be setting secrets in sequence)
- `esk deploy` — full cross-field validation. All secrets for the environment are available. Fail on violations
- `esk status` — report cross-field violations as warnings

## Error output example

```
✗ Validation failed for environment "prod":
  Cross-field errors:
    AUTH_ENABLED is "true" but AUTH_SECRET is not set
    AUTH_ENABLED is "true" but AUTH_ISSUER_URL is not set
    OAUTH_CLIENT_ID is set but OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET is not set
    Neither DATABASE_URL nor DB_HOST is set — one is required
```

## Implementation notes

- Depends on value validation schemas (esk-xxx) being implemented first — extends the `validate` block syntax
- Cross-field validation runs as a second pass after individual field validation
- All referenced secret keys in rules must exist in config (catch typos early)
- Rules reference secrets by key name, resolved per-environment (so `required_if: { AUTH_ENABLED: "true" }` checks AUTH_ENABLED's value in the same environment)
- Start simple: implement `required_if` and `required_with` first. `required_unless` and groups can come later
- The validation engine should build a dependency graph and detect circular references at config parse time

## Prior art

- t3-env `createFinalSchema`: Full discriminated union via Zod transform — extremely expressive but requires TypeScript. Example: `if (env.SKIP_AUTH) return { SKIP_AUTH: true }; if (!env.EMAIL || !env.PASSWORD) { addIssue(...); return z.NEVER; }`
- Laravel validation: `required_if:field,value`, `required_with:field1,field2`, `required_unless:field,value` — well-established patterns from PHP
- Rails validates: `validates :password, presence: true, if: :auth_enabled?`
- JSON Schema: `dependentRequired` keyword — if property A is present, properties B and C must also be present

title: generate: treat empty strings as missing in runtime validator
description:
Both t3-env (emptyStringAsUndefined) and arkenv handle the common case where a .env file has KEY= (empty string). The current runtime validator checks value === undefined || value === "".

This already works, but could be made configurable if users want to allow empty strings as valid values. For now this is just documenting the behavior and ensuring it's correct. Low priority unless users request configurability.

title: Add ReconcileOutcome summary type
description: reconcile.rs — Callers must interpret action, local_changed, has_drift, sources_to_update in combination. Add a higher-level method like result.needs_local_write() -> bool to reduce interpretation burden in sync.rs.

title: Remove dead newline escape code in dotenv format_env_value
description: targets/dotenv.rs:25-53 — validate_dotenv_value rejects newlines, but format_env_value has code to escape them. Since validation runs first, the escape path is dead code. Remove it to eliminate confusion.

title: Use enum for Cloudflare target mode instead of string comparison
description: targets/cloudflare.rs:103 — Compares self.target_config.mode == "pages" as string. Create CloudflareMode enum (Workers | Pages) to catch typos at parse time.

title: Truncate multi-line stderr in CommandOutput::check errors
description: targets/mod.rs:90-96 — Error format is "{command} failed for {key}: {stderr}". Multi-line stderr from CLI tools produces unwieldy errors. Truncate to first/last line for display, log full output at verbose level.

title: Align codebase with coding guidelines
description:
Audit of src/ against docs/coding-guidelines.md found 13 improvement areas, ordered by impact.

## High impact

### 1. `DeployResult` uses `success: bool, error: Option<String>` — the exact anti-pattern from the guidelines
`src/targets/mod.rs:22-28` — Should be an enum:
```rust
pub enum DeployOutcome { Success, Failed(String) }
pub struct DeployResult { pub key: String, pub target: ResolvedTarget, pub outcome: DeployOutcome }
```
Callers in `src/cli/deploy.rs:568,577` pattern-match on `result.success` + `result.error.clone().unwrap_or_default()` — would become cleaner with match arms.

### 2. `target_names()` — 14 identical if-chains → data-driven
`src/config.rs:922-967` — Pure data expressed as 14 separate `if self.targets.X.is_some() { names.push("X") }` branches. The guidelines show this exact refactor:
```rust
[("env", self.targets.env.is_some()), ("cloudflare", self.targets.cloudflare.is_some()), ...]
    .into_iter().filter(|(_, present)| *present).map(|(name, _)| name).collect()
```

### 3. `target_candidates()` / `remote_candidates()` — repetitive factory blocks
- `src/targets/mod.rs:206-363` — 13 identical `if let Some(cfg) = ... { push(TargetCandidate { ... }) }` blocks
- `src/remotes/mod.rs:126-233` — 8 identical blocks for remotes
- Adding a new target/remote requires touching 3+ places

### 4. `delete::run()` takes two positional bools
`src/cli/delete.rs:11,15` — `(no_sync: bool, bail: bool)` produces opaque call sites like `run_with_runner(&config, "MY_SECRET", "dev", false, false, &runner)`. Should be a `DeleteOptions` struct matching the pattern used by `deploy::run()` with `DeployOptions`.

### 5. Missing clippy pedantic lints
`src/lib.rs` has no lint attributes. The guidelines specify:
```rust
#![warn(clippy::pedantic)]
#![allow(clippy::module_name_repetitions)]
#![allow(clippy::must_use_candidate)]
#![allow(clippy::match_same_arms)]
```

## Medium impact

### 6. Free functions that should be methods
- `src/config.rs:1119` — `secret_group_names(config: &Config)` → `Config::secret_group_names(&self)`
- `src/remotes/mod.rs:25` — `extract_env_secrets(payload, env)` → `StorePayload::extract_env_secrets(&self, env)`
- `src/orphan.rs:32` — `detect(index, resolved, filter)` → `DeployIndex::detect_orphans(...)`

### 7. Unnecessary allocations for comparisons
- `src/config.rs:455` — `self.environments.contains(&env.to_string())` → `.iter().any(|e| e == env)`
- `src/cli/list.rs:80` — `targeted.contains(&(key.to_string(), e.to_string()))` — two `String` allocs per cell
- `src/cli/sync.rs:349` — `sources_to_update.contains(&p.name().to_string())`

### 8. Tail-return violation
`src/cli/status.rs:27-29` — `dashboard.render(all)?; Ok(())` should be `Dashboard::build(config, env, runner)?.render(all)`

### 9. Push loops replaceable with iterator chains
- `src/reconcile.rs:189,208` — filter+push loop → `.filter().map().collect()`

## Low impact

### 10. `get_` prefix on getter
`src/remotes/onepassword.rs:63` — `get_item` → `item` or `fetch_item`

### 11. Import ordering
Every file interleaves external crates and `std` alphabetically. Guidelines say external first, `std` second. The `crate::` separation is correct.

### 12. `Vec::with_capacity` opportunities
`target_names()`, `sync.rs` line builders, `status.rs` summary parts — small vecs in non-hot paths.

### 13. Duplicated `target_display()` logic
`src/config.rs:972` and `src/orphan.rs:19` have identical implementations.

title: Implement ZeroizeOnDrop for SecretValue
description: targets/mod.rs:57-62 — SecretValue carries plaintext secret values, derives Clone, and doesn't implement Zeroize. Add ZeroizeOnDrop (at least for the value field). Audit Clone usage and prefer references where possible.
close_reason: Won't fix: ZeroizeOnDrop on SecretValue is security theater. By construction time, plaintext exists in multiple non-zeroized allocations (serde JSON, BTreeMap, clones). Zeroing one copy while others remain creates false confidence. The master key is already properly zeroized in store.rs.

title: generate: type coercion hints from config
description:
arkenv's standout feature is automatic type coercion (string→number, string→boolean). esk could support a type field on SecretDef:

  secrets:
    Config:
      PORT:
        type: number
        targets:
          env: [web:dev]
      DEBUG:
        type: boolean
        targets:
          env: [web:dev]

In .d.ts mode: generate PORT: number instead of PORT: string.
In runtime mode: generate coercion logic (parseInt, boolean parsing).
In Zod mode: generate z.coerce.number(), z.coerce.boolean().

This adds real value over plain string typing without requiring users to adopt a separate validation library.

title: Security: high priority findings
description: Key material stored as plain Vec<u8> instead of Zeroizing wrappers. Two findings: master key field and key-producing functions.

title: Support array format for env_flags
description: targets/mod.rs:198-204 — env_flags split by whitespace cannot handle values with spaces. Support array format as alternative: env_flags: { prod: ["--env", "production"] }. Document that esk.yaml is a trusted config file.
close_reason: Resolved as part of esk-7pk security review

title: Deprecate StorePayload.version as decision-making field
description: reconcile.rs:124 — Global version is vestigial once per-env versioning is active. It's a high-water mark, not causal. Document this, consider deprecating it for cross-env reasoning to reduce cognitive overhead.

title: Secret validation and environment auditing
description: Inspired by t3-env and arkenv: add value validation schemas, empty string detection, required-variable auditing, orphaned secret detection, and conditional validation rules to catch bad/missing/stale values before they reach production. These tools solve application-layer env var validation (typed, schema-validated access to process.env). esk operates one layer below — encrypted storage, deployment, and sync. Several of their ideas translate directly to esk's deploy and status workflows.
